title: Family Office AI Skill Manifest
description: Schema for entries in skills/catalog.yaml and for optional per-skill manifests. This schema is documentation and optional structure only. It is NOT enforced by any script in this repository, which is non-executable by default. It encodes the conservative, read-only, human-in-the-loop posture every skill must follow.

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier matching the skill folder name.

category.description: Operating-model layer or functional area the skill serves.

description.description: Concise description of what the skill produces. Must describe a draft for review, not a decision.

intended_users.description: Roles the skill is designed for.

input_types.description: Kinds of input the skill consumes.

output_types.description: Kinds of draft artifact the skill produces.

risk_level.description: Sensitivity of the domain the skill touches. Describes domain sensitivity, not a guarantee of safety. Skills touching wealth, investment, legal, tax, or family office operations are typically 'medium' even when read-only.

tool_permissions.description: Tool/system access the skill is granted. Must be 'none'. Skills in this repository are read-only and access no live systems.

context_sources.description: How context may be provided to the skill — for example, manual input, or an approved read-only connector the user separately configures and governs. These are not bundled integrations; the repository includes no connectors. See docs/connectors-and-context.md.

connector_posture.description: The conservative posture for any connector used to feed this skill: manual input or approved read-only connector; no write, send, or transaction authority.

human_review_required.description: Whether a competent person must review output before use. Must be true.

prohibited_inputs.description: Inputs that must never be provided to the skill.

prohibited_actions.description: Actions the skill must never take. The listed core actions are required in every manifest.

advice_boundary.description: Plain statement of where the skill stops and professional advice begins.

example_use_case.description: A short, fictional, generic example of how the skill is used.

path.description: Repository-relative path to the skill folder.
